content.full_text:
MEMBERS OF THE CITY COUNCIL
KENNETH E FRIESS
JERRY HARRIS
G RY L HAUSOORFEP
GIL JONES
.JEFF VASOUEZ
SYEPHEN B JULIAN
October 2, 1992
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento,CA 95804-0807
Dear Commissioner:
My recent marriage on September 3, 1992, has raised the question of a potential conflict
of interest pursuant to the Political Reform Act. I would appreciate your written opinion
to the two questions listed below.
1. 
Can I, Douglas D. Dumharg employee for the City of San Juan Capistrano,
administer the City's Franchise Agreement with Solag Disposal, who employs my
wife, Julie Ann Bennitt Dumhart?
Z. 
Can I, Douglas D. Dumhar! be project manager for relocation of the City's solid
waste hauler, Solag Disposal, who employs my wife, Julie Ann Bennitt Dumhart?
FACTS
Contractual Services - The City of San Juan Capistrano has a seven-year exclusive franchise
agreement with Solag Disposal Company, Incorporated, for solid waste collection services.
This comprehensive agreement provides for integrated solid waste management services.
Solag Disposal provides automated residential curbside refuse collection, recycling and
composting commercial and industrial waste collection, public education and information,
AB939 material tracking and reporting, as well as assistance with implementing the City's
State-mandated Source Reduction and Recycling Element. Pursuant to the conditions of
the franchise agreement, Solag Disposal is to employ a recycling coordinator to provide
much of the above-referenced services.
Relocation Effort - ln 1974, the City council approved the city's General Plan which
identified the need for a specific plan for the State's oldest neighborhood, the Los Rios
area. In 1978, the city council adopted the Los Rios Precise Plan. The Precise Plan
identified the development of a Historic Park site. Solag Disposal company has been
operating at 3L735 Los Rios Street in the l,os Rios area since 1971 under a land use permit.
324oo pAsEo ADELANTo, sAN JUAN cAPlsrRANo, cALIFoRNIA 92575 ' (114) 493'1171
e
r9
ri)


California Fair Political Practices Commission
October 2, 1992
Page 2
The Precise Plan contained no specific discussion of the Solag Disposal use, but it does
identifr a majority of the Solag Disposal property to be used for the proposed Historic Park
site. The Precise Plan is silent on the process for acquiring the Solag Disposal site and is
deficient on an economic or action plan for City acquisition of the property. In 1988, the
San Juan Capistrano Community Redevelopment Agency purchased land at the south end
of the City limits. The Agency then entered into an agreement on March 6, 1990, with Tom
Trulis, owner of Solag Disposal, to exchange properties to facilitate the development of the
Los Rios Historic Park.
Staff Background - The City of San Juan Capistrano personnel hierarchy is arranged into
a three-tier structure. They are the classified, management and executive groups. The
largest group, classified, are all non-management personnel including many supervisory
positions. Management employees are the middle management positions which are
primarily Department Head chief assistants. The executive group is composed of the
Departrnent Heads.
In October of 1990, I receive a promotion from a classified position in the City's
Community Planning and Development Department to a management position in the City
Manager's Office to spearhead the City's compliance with the California Integrate Waste
Management Act of 1989 (AB939). My scope of work includes handling any issue with
respect to waste management such as administering the Solid Waste Franchise Agreement
project management for the relocation of the City's waste hauler, and implementing the
City's Source Reduction and Recycling Element and Household Hazardous Waste Element.
Solag Disposal Company employs Julie Ann Bennitt Dumhar! my wife, part-time as a
recycling coordinator. Her responsibilities include presentations to civic groups, public
education campaigns, customer service for curbside recycling and report generation for
AB939 requirements.
To further assist your analysis and develop a conclusion to my questions, I have included
copies of our franchise and exchange agreements. If I can provide any additional
information or answer questions, please call me at (714) 493-ll7l, extension 333.
Sincerely,
@/2-Z/-
Douglas D. Dumhart
Management Assistant I
rmb
enc

sections.question:
1. Can I, Douglas D. Dumharg employee for the City of San Juan Capistrano, administer the City's Franchise Agreement with Solag Disposal, who employs my wife, Julie Ann Bennitt Dumhart?
2. Can I, Douglas D. Dumhar! be project manager for relocation of the City's solid waste hauler, Solag Disposal, who employs my wife, Julie Ann Bennitt Dumhart?

sections.question_synthetic: Can a city employee administer a franchise agreement with, and serve as project manager for the relocation of, a waste disposal company that employs his spouse following their recent marriage?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The requester seeks advice on two specific conflict of interest questions related to the Political Reform Act. Document contains detailed factual background about the city's franchise agreement, relocation efforts, staff hierarchy, and the requester's role and spouse's employment.

embedding.summary: Douglas D. Dumhart, a Management Assistant I for the City of San Juan Capistrano, requests written FPPC advice on potential conflicts of interest arising from his recent marriage to Julie Ann Bennitt Dumhart, who works part-time for Solag Disposal—a company with which the city has a waste management franchise agreement and an ongoing property relocation project that Dumhart oversees.
